channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq futures rise after a strong week on Wall Street, led by chip stocks: Live updates
item.description: The Dow climbed nearly 2% last week, putting it within striking distance of 53,000, a level it has never reached.

item.title: Versant agrees to buy golf simulator company Full Swing for $530 million
item.description: The deal for Full Swing will expand Versant's nontraditional media assets in an effort to diversify revenue from cable television.

item.title: TeraWulf shares soar after Anthropic leases data center in Kentucky
item.description: TeraWulf is a crytpo mining company that has pivoted to AI data center infrastructure. Its stock is up more than 80% this year.

item.title: Belgium set to appeal as FIFA reverses Balogun's World Cup suspension after Trump reportedly intervenes
item.description: Folarin Balogun was surprisingly cleared by FIFA to play for the United States Men's National Team in the World Cup against Belgium on Monday.

item.title: Here are Monday's biggest analyst calls: Nvidia, Netflix, AMD, First Solar, Micron, Sandisk, T-Mobile & more
item.description: Here are Monday's biggest calls on Wall Street.

item.title: Lockheed Martin to buy naval defense firm Ultra Maritime for $3.45 billion
item.description: Ultra is owned by private equity firm Advent International, and specialises in anti-submarine technology.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: Lam Research, Intel, DataDog & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves premarket.

item.title: Trump's calls, Ukraine's strikes and Russia's barrage on Kyiv put markets on alert
item.description: Trump reportedly spoke with Putin and Zelenskyy as Ukraine struck Russian targets and Moscow launched another deadly attack on Kyiv.

item.title: Why Iran may find it difficult to clear its oil inventories even after sanctions relief
item.description: Iran may face challenges in clearing oil inventories even after restrictions have been lifted, amid oil supplies elsewhere while China becomes less enthusiastic

item.title: Feds seek lower prison term for $100M New Jersey deli fraudster — but some reasons why are hidden
item.description: James Patten is the third person who will be sentenced in the stock manipulation scheme related to Hometown International, a company that owned just one deli.

item.title: Investing in 'Trump Accounts': Here's what you need to know
item.description: CNBC’s Sharon Epperson discusses ‘Trump Accounts.' To learn more, sign up for the Money 101 newsletter with Sharon Epperson.

item.title: Record heat, crowds drive offseason boom in international travel
item.description: Airlines are extending flight schedules to maximize on lucrative international travel.

item.title: ITV and Sky reshape British TV landscape with $2.1 billion deal
item.description: Sky CEO Dana Strong said the deal was a "defining moment" in British broadcasting.

item.title: Adidas is more than a one-off World Cup trade, says UBS. Here are 3 reasons why
item.description: UBS spelled out three reasons why Adidas is primed for longer-term growth beyond the World Cup.

item.title: As ACA enrollment falls by millions, Trump administration and policy gurus disagree on why
item.description: Affordable Care Act marketplace enrollment has declined by 3 million people. The Trump administration points to fraud controls. Policy experts point to cost.

item.title: First known congressional SpaceX stock buys surface after record IPO
item.description: The SpaceX stock purchases come as Elon Musk’s company deepens its role in federal contracting and remains closely tied to President Donald Trump’s Washington.

item.title: Nvidia's next-gen AI rack system delayed to 2028 on manufacturing snags, SemiAnalysis says
item.description: The reported delay adds to concerns that Nvidia's breakneck annual release cadence is colliding with manufacturing limits.

item.title: Meta’s woes deepen in India as child abuse ads on Instagram draw government ire
item.description: U.S. social media giant Meta is facing an onslaught of regulatory scrutiny in India, which has the largest user base for Facebook, Instagram and WhatsApp.

item.title: Cargo vessel in Red Sea reports coming under attack, UK maritime body says
item.description: The incident was reported in one of the world's most crucial trade transit routes amid a fragile ceasefire between Iran and the U.S.

item.title: We're buying more shares of Jim Cramer's new favorite chip stock
item.description: Shares are trading around the same price as our previous purchase in mid-June.

item.title: EasyJet shares soar 10% as budget airline agrees to $7.3 billion Castlelake takeover
item.description: EasyJet shares surged in early dealmaking Monday after it agreed in principle to takeover bid from Castlelake.

item.title: How much you should have in your savings account — and the right bank to choose
item.description: Not sure if you have enough (or too much) in your savings account? How to find the right balance —and the right bank.

item.title: Don't throw away your shot to speak to an AI Alexander Hamilton at Boston's new finance museum
item.description: Nearly a decade after leaving its former home, the Museum of American Finance is opening its new doors to the public.

item.title: 'NATO 3.0': Defense spending pledges face the Trump test
item.description: Leaders are expected to focus on whether Europe can turn higher defense spending into military power as Washington pushes allies to shoulder more of the burden.

item.title: Wells Fargo says this struggling solar stock to benefit from potential trade policy shift
item.description: The investment bank has an overweight rating on the solar energy name.

item.title: This semiconductor stock has more than doubled in a year. Bernstein sees more gains ahead
item.description: The firm reiterated its outperform rating on the stock and hiked its price target.

item.title: Ebola outbreak containment hampered by USAID closure, experts say
item.description: Foreign aid cuts have "demonstrably worsened" the Ebola crisis, a virologist told CNBC.

item.title: South Korean prosecutors indict four major refiners over allegations of oil price collusion
item.description: South Korean prosecutors on Monday indicted four major domestic oil refiners for violating fair trade laws.

item.title: Brides are bringing back the one-night-only bachelorette party: 'You just come, have one perfect night and leave'
item.description: Before they ballooned into multi-day destination getaways, bachelorette parties tended to be one night of raucous fun. Some brides are bringing it back.

item.title: The World Cup sends prediction market volumes soaring to record highs
item.description: Trading volumes on Kalshi and Polymarket both hit record highs in June. Rothera, a new prediction market platform, was also able to manage $2 billion in volume.
